title: The best new science-fiction books of April 2026
summary: A collection of stories set in George R. R. Martin’s Wild Cards universe and a novel from The Expanse author James S. A. Corey are among the science-fiction books we’re looking forward to this month

title: الحرب تسرع الاتجاه للاستثمار في الطاقة المتجددة
summary: تربك حرب إيران خارطة إمدادات الغاز، بما يدفع الدول المستوردة للبحث عن بدائل لتأمين احتياجاتها للطهي والتدفئة وتوليد الكهرباء، بينما تستعد الولايات المتحدة، كأكبر مصدر للغاز في العالم، للاستفادة من هذا الاضطراب على المدى القصير.

title: وزراء طاقة أوروبا يبحثون تنسيق الاستجابة لتداعيات حرب إيران
summary: يستعد وزراء الطاقة في الاتحاد الأوروبي لعقد اجتماع طارئ غداً الثلاثاء، لمناقشة سبل تنسيق الاستجابة للاضطرابات التي تشهدها أسواق الطاقة العالمية بفعل الحرب مع إيران، وفقاً لوثيقة اطلعت عليها رويترز.

title: I almost drowned in space when my helmet filled with water
summary: During his second-ever spacewalk, European Space Agency astronaut Luca Parmitano felt water creeping across his face – and knew he could be moments from drowning inside his helmet

title: We could protect Earth from dangerous asteroids using a huge magnet
summary: A new spacecraft concept called NOVA could keep asteroids from hitting our planet by using a huge magnet to gradually pull them apart while shifting their trajectories

title: Author of Red Mars calls 'bullshit' on emigrating to the planet
summary: Kim Stanley Robinson opens his classic science fiction novel Red Mars in 2026. As the New Scientist Book Club embarks on reading it in April, he looks back on its origins – and how the idea of moving to Mars holds up today

title: Why Kim Stanley Robinson's Red Mars is still a classic, 34 years on
summary: As the New Scientist Book Club reads Kim Stanley Robinson’s science-fiction novel in April, George Bass digs into why this 1992 book still feels so relevant today

title: Private company to land on asteroid Apophis as it flies close to Earth
summary: Apophis will be visited by multiple spacecraft – including landers – when it skims past Earth in three years

title: Captivating space images show how it has inspired us through the ages
summary: An upcoming book from presenter and author Dallas Campbell collects both iconic and lesser-known images of space, from illustration to photography

title: Maggie Aderin's dream: To walk by the footprints of Neil Armstrong
summary: Space scientist Maggie Aderin talks telescopes, neurodiversity and being underestimated with Rowan Hooper on the New Scientist podcast, as her memoir Starchild comes out
